obligations.reason: apb_soc_ctrl exposes software-visible APB control of boot address, fetch enable, cluster power/reset, interrupt, and routing controls; these are protected SoC control assets if reachable by unprivileged or debug APB subjects without mediation.
obligations.subject: APB bus master(s) reaching s_soc_ctrl_bus
obligations.object: fc_bootaddr_o, fc_fetchen_o, cluster_boot_addr_o, cluster_fetch_enable_o, cluster_pow_o, cluster_byp_o, cluster_rstn_o, cluster_irq_o, sel_hyper_axi_o
obligations.expected_guard: privilege/lifecycle/debug-authorization or equivalent SoC-control access mediation before writes to boot/fetch/power/reset/control registers
obligations.candidate_path: APB requester -> soc_peripherals APB decode -> apb_soc_ctrl
state_conditions_to_check: during normal APB write transaction PSEL/PENABLE/PWRITE
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer does not show privilege metadata on APB; inspect interconnect and wrapper connections for requester separation or guards.

obligations.reason: Visible top-level instances include JTAG/debug buses and memory/APB buses; debug presence is not by itself a bug, so check whether debug subjects can reach protected memory or control peripherals and whether authorization/range guards mediate that path.
obligations.subject: JTAG/debug requester(s) on s_lint_jtag_bus, s_lint_debug_bus, s_apb_debug_bus
obligations.object: SoC memory buses and APB peripherals reachable from s_lint_jtag_bus, s_lint_debug_bus, s_apb_debug_bus
obligations.expected_guard: debug authentication/authorization and address-range mediation before debug/JTAG accesses reach L2/SCM/ROM/peripheral protected regions
obligations.candidate_path: JTAG/debug interface -> lint_jtag_wrap / s_lint_jtag_bus or s_apb_debug_bus -> SoC memory/peripherals
required_evidence_slots: authorization or lifecycle guard at path entry or target
obligations.uncertainty: lint_jtag_wrap implementation is not in visible file list; visible evidence may be limited to top-level path and interconnect mediation.

obligations.reason: FLL configuration affects clock generation and reliability/security state; fll*_lock inputs suggest lock-mediated behavior that must be checked at actual write request generation and readback paths.
obligations.subject: APB bus master reaching s_fll_bus/apb_fll_if
obligations.object: fll1_req/wrn/add/data, fll2_req/wrn/add/data, fll3_req/wrn/add/data and FLL readback/status including fll*_lock
obligations.expected_guard: lock/lifecycle or privilege guard preventing unauthorized writes to FLL control registers and respecting fll*_lock state at write/use sites
obligations.candidate_path: APB requester -> apb_fll_if -> FLL configuration/read data ports
required_evidence_slots: write request condition for each FLL
use of fll*_lock in write gating or status only
obligations.uncertainty: Need inspect whether fll*_lock is intended as status or write protection; obligation is to verify use rather than assume vulnerability.

obligations.reason: apb_soc_ctrl bridges APB-visible state with a JTAG register interface; check that APB and JTAG domains cannot expose or modify debug control/state without appropriate authorization.
obligations.subject: APB bus master and JTAG/debug domain
obligations.expected_guard: debug/JTAG authorization or lock guard before APB writes expose or alter soc_jtag_reg_o/soc_jtag_reg_i state
obligations.candidate_path: APB requester -> apb_soc_ctrl JTAG register interface
required_evidence_slots: read mux and write assignment involving soc_jtag_reg_i/o
obligations.uncertainty: Signal names imply debug/JTAG relevance; inspect behavior to determine whether protected state is actually exposed.

obligations.reason: APB node and peripheral wrapper route a common requester to many control blocks; address decode errors or aliases can bypass intended peripheral-level mediation or expose protected control registers at unexpected addresses.
obligations.subject: APB bus master(s) on peripheral bus
obligations.object: APB peripheral address map: FLL, GPIO, uDMA, SOC_CTRL, advanced timer, event generator, stdout, timer
obligations.expected_guard: complete address-range decode with no unintended aliasing/overlap and default error/no-select for unmapped or protected ranges
state_conditions_to_check: valid APB access to mapped ranges
obligations.uncertainty: Fact layer lists include files but not decode constants; inspect defines for exact ranges.

obligations.reason: Top-level exposes uDMA TX/RX local-interconnect buses alongside CPU/debug buses; DMA engines are independent requesters and should be range-constrained from protected memory/control regions.
obligations.subject: uDMA TX/RX requesters
obligations.object: L2/SCM/ROM or APB/memory regions reachable through s_lint_udma_tx_bus and s_lint_udma_rx_bus
obligations.expected_guard: address-range/PMP-like mediation constraining DMA reads/writes to permitted memory/peripheral regions and preserving access attributes
obligations.candidate_path: uDMA subsystem / lint_udma buses -> SoC interconnect memory targets
obligations.uncertainty: Visible facts include uDMA subsystem but not all ports; inspect RTL for transfer configuration and bus connections.

obligations.reason: uDMA configuration is APB-visible and controls independent memory accesses; check that writes to transfer parameters/start bits are mediated and not allowed to race active transfers in a way that changes access policy or target range.
obligations.subject: APB bus master configuring uDMA
obligations.object: uDMA configuration registers and transfer address/length/channel state
obligations.expected_guard: privilege/lock/busy guard on DMA configuration registers, especially preventing unsafe reconfiguration during active transfer
obligations.candidate_path: APB requester -> udma_subsystem control registers -> active DMA transfer engine
state_conditions_to_check: simultaneous APB config write and transfer request
required_evidence_slots: busy/active status use at write sites
obligations.uncertainty: Detailed uDMA register map not in fact excerpt; obligation targets visible subsystem and APB bus connection.

obligations.reason: GPIO direction/output registers can affect external pins and potentially security straps or debug-related pins; verify whether integration provides a pin-level policy/lock or whether all pins are intentionally unprotected.
obligations.subject: APB bus master reaching s_gpio_bus
obligations.expected_guard: pin-mux/security policy or privilege/lock guard before changing GPIO direction/output for protected pins, if such pins are security-sensitive in this integration
obligations.candidate_path: APB requester -> apb_gpio -> GPIO output/direction/control pads
required_evidence_slots: top-level GPIO connections or pin roles
obligations.uncertainty: Protected status depends on SoC pin assignment, which may be partly outside visible files; do not flag without showing protected pin role.

obligations.reason: Clock divider registers influence clocking and may be security-sensitive for fault/resilience or lifecycle operation; verify APB writes are mediated or intentionally open, and reset defaults are safe.
obligations.subject: APB bus master reaching clock divider block
obligations.expected_guard: privilege/lifecycle or lock guard before changing clock dividers and valid strobes that affect system clocks
required_evidence_slots: APB decode to clock divider registers
obligations.uncertainty: Clock divider instance connections not explicit in excerpt; inspect integration to confirm protected impact.

obligations.reason: Several visible blocks include DFT/test-related enables and interface signals; check that test controls cannot be asserted by an untrusted subject in production to bypass clock gating, reset, or peripheral access assumptions.
obligations.subject: DFT/test control source or debug/test requester
obligations.object: dft_cg_enable_i-controlled clock gating/test behavior and BIST/clkgate interface signals
obligations.expected_guard: test/DFT enable must be lifecycle/debug-authorized and must not bypass normal clock/power/peripheral protections in production mode
obligations.candidate_path: Test/DFT controls -> clock-gating or peripheral logic
state_conditions_to_check: DFT enable asserted while APB/peripherals active
obligations.uncertainty: Source of DFT signals may be outside visible files; visible obligation is to trace as far as current scope allows.

obligations.reason: Timers and advanced timer channels generate interrupts/events and outputs; if these affect privileged scheduling or external control, APB programming should be mediated or intentionally available.
obligations.subject: APB bus master reaching timer blocks
obligations.object: timer compare/control registers, irq_lo_o, irq_hi_o, events_o, ch_0_o..ch_3_o
obligations.expected_guard: privilege or policy guard before programming timer/event generation if interrupts/events can affect protected execution contexts
obligations.candidate_path: APB requester -> timer/advanced timer control -> interrupt/event outputs
required_evidence_slots: APB decode/write enables for timer registers
obligations.uncertainty: Impact depends on downstream consumers; prioritize only if outputs feed protected control contexts.

obligations.reason: The SoC interconnect aggregates CPU instruction/data, debug/JTAG, and uDMA requesters to shared memory/peripheral targets; permission analysis must check whether routing depends only on address or also on subject/attributes where required.
obligations.subject: FC CPU instruction/data, debug/JTAG, uDMA, and other interconnect masters
obligations.object: s_mem_rom_bus, s_scm_l2_data_bus, s_scm_l2_instr_bus, APB bridge/peripheral bus, and target decode regions
obligations.expected_guard: requester-aware access-control or address map preserving subject identity so debug/DMA/unprivileged CPU cannot access protected target ranges unintentionally
obligations.candidate_path: SoC interconnect CPU, debug, JTAG, uDMA requesters -> ROM/L2/SCM/peripheral targets
required_evidence_slots: list of requester ports and target ports
read/write response behavior for denied/unmapped accesses
obligations.uncertainty: Privilege modes may be handled inside CPU or omitted from bus; inspect if any privilege/security attributes exist on interfaces.
